name: LLM-Powered Universal Ingestion
overview: Replace per-format adapters with a single LLM-powered ingestion endpoint that accepts up to 5 files of any type, extracts contacts and interactions from their text, and calls existing graph write tools to populate Neo4j.
todos:
  - id: text-extraction
    content: Add text extraction helpers for common file types (CSV, EML, ICS, PDF, TXT) in backend/services/file_extraction.py
    status: pending
  - id: ingest-endpoint
    content: New POST /api/connections/import-smart endpoint accepting up to 5 files + member_name; orchestrates extraction → LLM → graph writes
    status: pending
  - id: llm-orchestrator
    content: New backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py — calls LLM with file text + tool schema, executes tool calls in a loop, returns summary
    status: pending
  - id: graph-write-tools
    content: Add low-level upsert_contact and upsert_interaction functions in ingestion_service.py as LLM-callable primitives
    status: pending
  - id: scoring-provenance
    content: Add sources[], last_interaction_at, interaction_count to KNOWS edges so scoring reflects email/calendar signals
    status: pending
  - id: frontend-uploader
    content: Rename/extend CSVUploader.tsx to accept up to 5 files of any extension; show per-file progress and LLM extraction summary
    status: pending
isProject: false
---

# LLM-Powered Universal Ingestion

## Core idea

Instead of writing a parser per file format, send the file's **text content** to the LLM with a **tool schema** (`upsert_contact`, `upsert_interaction`). The LLM reads the content, understands its structure, and calls those tools to populate the graph. One endpoint handles LinkedIn CSV, EML, ICS, forwarded emails pasted as TXT, PDF contact lists, etc.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  user["User uploads 1-5 files\n(any extension)"] --> endpoint["POST /api/connections/import-smart\nmember_name + files[]"]
  endpoint --> extract["file_extraction.py\nextract_text(file)"]
  extract --> llm["LLM\nOpenRouter / Gemini\n(existing _call_openai_compatible)"]
  llm -->|"tool call: upsert_contact"| uc["ingestion_service.py\nupsert_contact()"]
  llm -->|"tool call: upsert_interaction"| ui["ingestion_service.py\nupsert_interaction()"]
  uc --> neo4j["Neo4j\nPerson · Company · KNOWS · WORKS_AT"]
  ui --> neo4j
  neo4j --> score["scoring_service.py\nrecompute_strength"]
  llm -->|"text: summary"| resp["JSON response\n{imported, merged, summary}"]
```

## What the LLM receives

One message per file block:

```
You are a network intelligence data extractor. The team member is: {member_name}.

Extract every person this team member has a relationship with.
For each person, call upsert_contact or upsert_interaction.

--- FILE: linkedin.csv ---
First Name,Last Name,Company,...
...

--- FILE: meeting_notes.txt ---
Had intro call with James Sullivan (CFO @ Bain Capital) on 12 Apr 2026.
...
```

The LLM iterates tool calls (agentic loop) until it emits a text summary.

## New files

### `backend/services/file_extraction.py` (new)

Thin switch on file extension / MIME type, returns plain text:

- `.csv` / `.tsv` → decode UTF-8 BOM, return raw text (LLM reads columns naturally)
- `.txt` / `.md` → decode as-is
- `.eml` → `email.message_from_bytes`, extract `From`, `To`, `Cc`, `Date`, `Subject` headers + text body
- `.ics` → raw text (iCal is plain text; LLM reads `DTSTART`, `SUMMARY`, `ATTENDEE` lines)
- `.pdf` → `pypdf` (add to `requirements.txt`) — extract page text
- fallback → attempt UTF-8 decode; skip if binary

### `backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py` (new)

```python
async def smart_ingest(
    member_name: str,
    files: list[tuple[str, bytes]],  # (filename, raw_bytes)
    llm_api_key: str,
    base_url: str,
    model: str,
) -> dict:
    # 1. extract text from each file
    # 2. build combined prompt with file blocks
    # 3. define INGEST_TOOLS schema (upsert_contact, upsert_interaction)
    # 4. agentic loop: call LLM, execute tool calls, feed results back
    # 5. return {imported, merged, skipped, summary}
```

**Tool schema exposed to LLM:**

```python
INGEST_TOOLS = [
  {
    "name": "upsert_contact",
    "description": "Add or merge a person into the network graph.",
    "parameters": {
      "first_name": str, "last_name": str, "company": str,
      "role": str,  # optional
      "connected_on": str,  # ISO date, optional
      "email": str,  # optional, aids dedup
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "upsert_interaction",
    "description": "Record an email, meeting, or other touch point with a person.",
    "parameters": {
      "contact_name": str, "contact_company": str,
      "interaction_type": str,  # "email" | "meeting" | "call" | "other"
      "occurred_at": str,  # ISO date
      "notes": str,  # optional, brief — not stored raw
    }
  }
]
```

### New primitives in `backend/services/ingestion_service.py`

```python
async def upsert_contact(
    member_name: str, first_name: str, last_name: str,
    company: str, role: str = "", connected_on: str = "",
    email: str = "",
) -> str:  # "imported" | "merged"

async def upsert_interaction(
    member_name: str, contact_name: str, contact_company: str,
    interaction_type: str, occurred_at: str, notes: str = "",
) -> str:
```

These are the same Neo4j Cypher operations as `ingest_csv` but per-row, callable by name in the tool loop.

### `backend/routers/connections.py`

Add:
```python
@router.post("/import-smart")
async def import_smart(
    member_name: str = Form(...),
    files: list[UploadFile] = File(...),  # max 5 enforced
    _key: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
):
```

Raises `400` if more than 5 files. Delegates to `smart_ingest()`.

### `frontend/app/components/CSVUploader.tsx` → rename to `FileUploader.tsx`

- Accept up to 5 files, any extension (remove `.csv` filter).
- Show file list with remove buttons.
- On response, show `summary` string from LLM instead of just counts.
- Update import in `ChatInterface.tsx`.

## Scoring integration (same as before)

`backend/services/ingestion_service.py` — `upsert_interaction` sets:
- `sources` array on `KNOWS` (appends `interaction_type` if not already present)
- `last_interaction_at` (most recent `occurred_at`)
- `interaction_count` (incremented)

`backend/services/scoring_service.py` — add `interaction_frequency_score`:

```python
WEIGHTS = {
    "recency":          0.25,
    "seniority":        0.25,
    "shared_connections": 0.20,
    "interaction_freq": 0.10,
    "source_diversity": 0.20,
}
```

## File map

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/services/file_extraction.py` | new |
| `backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py` | new |
| `backend/services/ingestion_service.py` | add `upsert_contact`, `upsert_interaction` primitives; add provenance props |
| `backend/services/scoring_service.py` | add `interaction_freq` weight |
| `backend/routers/connections.py` | add `POST /import-smart` |
| `backend/requirements.txt` | add `pypdf` |
| `frontend/app/components/CSVUploader.tsx` | rename to `FileUploader.tsx`, accept any ext, up to 5 |
| `frontend/app/components/ChatInterface.tsx` | update import name |

No changes needed to: `graph_service.py`, `routers/graph.py`, path algorithms, MCP server (can add MCP tool later).

## Key decisions

- **LLM does the parsing** — no format-specific code per source; if a new export format appears, prompt engineering handles it.
- **Agentic tool loop** — same `_call_openai_compatible` already in `chat.py`; reused here, not duplicated.
- **No raw body stored** — LLM extracts names/dates/companies; we pass only `notes` (brief summary, not body text) to graph.
- **Graceful fallback** — if LLM is unavailable, the old `POST /import` (LinkedIn CSV only) still works unchanged.
- **Token safety** — each file text is capped at ~8 000 chars before sending to LLM; large files are chunked across multiple LLM calls.

content.text:
Here's the revised plan:

## What changes

Instead of writing a CSV adapter, then an EML adapter, then an ICS adapter… one LLM-powered endpoint handles every file format.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  user["User uploads 1-5 files\n(any extension)"] --> endpoint["POST /api/connections/import-smart\nmember_name + files"]
  endpoint --> extract["file_extraction.py\nextract_text per file"]
  extract --> llm["LLM\n(reuses _call_openai_compatible\nfrom chat.py)"]
  llm -->|"upsert_contact tool call"| uc["ingestion_service.py\nupsert_contact()"]
  llm -->|"upsert_interaction tool call"| ui["ingestion_service.py\nupsert_interaction()"]
  uc --> neo4j["Neo4j KNOWS / Person"]
  ui --> neo4j
  neo4j --> score["scoring_service.py\nrecompute_strength"]
  llm -->|text summary| resp["JSON {imported, merged, summary}"]
```

### New files
- **`backend/services/file_extraction.py`** — thin text extractor per extension (`.csv`, `.eml`, `.ics`, `.txt`, `.pdf` via `pypdf`; fallback UTF-8 decode).
- **`backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py`** — agentic loop: builds prompt with file text blocks, feeds LLM two tools (`upsert_contact`, `upsert_interaction`), executes tool calls, returns summary.

### Modified files
- **[`backend/services/ingestion_service.py`](backend/services/ingestion_service.py)** — add `upsert_contact()` and `upsert_interaction()` as per-row primitives the LLM calls; add `sources[]`, `last_interaction_at`, `interaction_count` to `KNOWS` edges.
- **[`backend/routers/connections.py`](backend/routers/connections.py)** — add `POST /api/connections/import-smart` (up to 5 files, any type).
- **[`backend/services/scoring_service.py`](backend/services/scoring_service.py)** — add `interaction_freq` weight (10%) fed by email/meeting counts.
- **[`backend/requirements.txt`](backend/requirements.txt)** — add `pypdf`.
- **[`frontend/app/components/CSVUploader.tsx`](frontend/app/components/CSVUploader.tsx)** — rename to `FileUploader.tsx`, remove `.csv` restriction, accept up to 5 files, show LLM summary on result.

### What stays the same
- Old `POST /api/connections/import` (LinkedIn CSV) still works unchanged.
- `graph_service.py`, path algorithms, MCP server — no changes.
- The existing `_call_openai_compatible` in `chat.py` is reused, not duplicated.

### Key guardrails
- File text capped at ~8 000 chars before sending to LLM (large files chunked across multiple calls).
- No raw email body stored — LLM extracts names/dates/companies only.
- If LLM is unavailable, old CSV endpoint is the fallback.

input.contents:
import csv
import io
import logging
from datetime import datetime

from slugify import slugify

from db.neo4j_client import get_driver

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

DATE_FORMATS = ["%d %b %Y", "%Y-%m-%d", "%m/%d/%Y", "%d/%m/%Y"]


def _parse_date(raw: str) -> str | None:
    raw = raw.strip()
    if not raw:
        return None
    for fmt in DATE_FORMATS:
        try:
            return datetime.strptime(raw, fmt).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        except ValueError:
            continue
    return None


def _make_id(first: str, last: str, company: str) -> str:
    return slugify(f"{first}_{last}_{company}", separator="_")


async def _ensure_member(session, member_name: str) -> None:
    await session.run(
        "MERGE (m:Person {full_name: $name}) "
        "ON CREATE SET m.id = $id, m.is_tsi_member = true, "
        "m.first_name = $first, m.last_name = $last",
        {
            "name": member_name,
            "id": slugify(member_name, separator="_"),
            "first": member_name.split()[0] if " " in member_name else member_name,
            "last": member_name.split()[-1] if " " in member_name else "",
        },
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Primitive: upsert a single contact (LLM-callable)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def upsert_contact(
    member_name: str,
    first_name: str,
    last_name: str,
    company: str,
    role: str = "",
    connected_on: str = "",
    email: str = "",
) -> str:
    """Add or merge a contact into the graph. Returns 'imported' or 'merged'."""
    if not first_name or not last_name or not company:
        return "skipped"

    parsed_date = _parse_date(connected_on) or "2020-01-01"
    person_id = _make_id(first_name, last_name, company)
    full_name = f"{first_name} {last_name}"

    driver = await get_driver()
    async with driver.session() as session:
        await _ensure_member(session, member_name)

        result = await session.run(
            """
            MERGE (p:Person {id: $id})
            ON CREATE SET
                p.first_name = $first_name,
                p.last_name = $last_name,
                p.full_name = $full_name,
                p.company = $company,
                p.role = $role,
                p.email = $email,
                p.is_tsi_member = false
            ON MATCH SET
                p.role = CASE WHEN $role <> '' THEN $role ELSE p.role END,
                p.email = CASE WHEN $email <> '' THEN $email ELSE p.email END
            WITH p,
                 CASE WHEN p.is_tsi_member IS NULL THEN false ELSE p.is_tsi_member END AS existed
            MERGE (c:Company {name: $company})
            MERGE (p)-[:WORKS_AT {role: $role, is_current: true}]->(c)
            WITH p, existed
            MATCH (m:Person {full_name: $member_name})
            MERGE (m)-[k:KNOWS]->(p)
            ON CREATE SET
                k.source_member = $member_name,
                k.connected_on = date($connected_on),
                k.shared_connection_count = 1,
                k.sources = ['linkedin'],
                k.last_interaction_at = date($connected_on),
                k.interaction_count = 0
            ON MATCH SET
                k.shared_connection_count = k.shared_connection_count + 1,
                k.sources = CASE
                    WHEN NOT 'linkedin' IN coalesce(k.sources, [])
                    THEN coalesce(k.sources, []) + ['linkedin']
                    ELSE k.sources
                END
            RETURN existed
            """,
            {
                "id": person_id,
                "first_name": first_name,
                "last_name": last_name,
                "full_name": full_name,
                "company": company,
                "role": role,
                "email": email,
                "member_name": member_name,
                "connected_on": parsed_date,
            },
        )
        records = [r async for r in result]
        existed = records[0].data().get("existed") if records else False
        await _recompute_scores(session, member_name)

    return "merged" if existed else "imported"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Primitive: upsert an interaction (email/meeting/call) (LLM-callable)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def upsert_interaction(
    member_name: str,
    contact_name: str,
    contact_company: str,
    interaction_type: str,
    occurred_at: str,
    notes: str = "",
) -> str:
    """Record an interaction between a member and a contact. Returns 'ok' or 'skipped'."""
    if not contact_name or not member_name:
        return "skipped"

    parsed_date = _parse_date(occurred_at) or datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    source_label = interaction_type.lower() if interaction_type else "other"

    driver = await get_driver()
    async with driver.session() as session:
        await _ensure_member(session, member_name)

        await session.run(
            """
            MATCH (m:Person {full_name: $member_name})
            MATCH (p:Person)
            WHERE toLower(p.full_name) CONTAINS toLower($contact_name)
              AND ($contact_company = '' OR toLower(p.company) CONTAINS toLower($contact_company))
            WITH m, p LIMIT 1
            MERGE (m)-[k:KNOWS]->(p)
            ON CREATE SET
                k.source_member = $member_name,
                k.connected_on = date($occurred_at),
                k.shared_connection_count = 1,
                k.sources = [$source_label],
                k.last_interaction_at = date($occurred_at),
                k.interaction_count = 1
            ON MATCH SET
                k.sources = CASE
                    WHEN NOT $source_label IN coalesce(k.sources, [])
                    THEN coalesce(k.sources, []) + [$source_label]
                    ELSE k.sources
                END,
                k.last_interaction_at = CASE
                    WHEN date($occurred_at) > coalesce(k.last_interaction_at, date('2000-01-01'))
                    THEN date($occurred_at)
                    ELSE k.last_interaction_at
                END,
                k.interaction_count = coalesce(k.interaction_count, 0) + 1
            """,
            {
                "member_name": member_name,
                "contact_name": contact_name,
                "contact_company": contact_company or "",
                "source_label": source_label,
                "occurred_at": parsed_date,
            },
        )
        await _recompute_scores(session, member_name)

    return "ok"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Batch CSV ingestion (existing, unchanged public API)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def ingest_csv(member_name: str, csv_text: str) -> dict:
    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(csv_text))

    rows = []
    for raw_row in reader:
        row = {k.strip(): v.strip() if v else "" for k, v in raw_row.items()}
        first = row.get("First Name", "")
        last = row.get("Last Name", "")
        company = row.get("Company", "")
        if not first or not last or not company:
            continue
        rows.append(
            {
                "id": _make_id(first, last, company),
                "first_name": first,
                "last_name": last,
                "full_name": f"{first} {last}",
                "company": company,
                "role": row.get("Position", ""),
                "connected_on": _parse_date(row.get("Connected On", "")),
            }
        )

    if not rows:
        return {"imported": 0, "merged": 0, "message": "No valid rows found"}

    driver = await get_driver()
    imported = 0
    merged = 0

    async with driver.session() as session:
        await _ensure_member(session, member_name)

        for row in rows:
            result = await session.run(
                """
                MERGE (p:Person {id: $id})
                ON CREATE SET
                    p.first_name = $first_name,
                    p.last_name = $last_name,
                    p.full_name = $full_name,
                    p.company = $company,
                    p.role = $role,
                    p.is_tsi_member = false
                ON MATCH SET
                    p.role = CASE WHEN $role <> '' THEN $role ELSE p.role END
                WITH p,
                     CASE WHEN p.is_tsi_member IS NULL THEN false ELSE p.is_tsi_member END AS existed
                MERGE (c:Company {name: $company})
                MERGE (p)-[:WORKS_AT {role: $role, is_current: true}]->(c)
                WITH p, existed
                MATCH (m:Person {full_name: $member_name})
                MERGE (m)-[k:KNOWS]->(p)
                ON CREATE SET
                    k.source_member = $member_name,
                    k.connected_on = date($connected_on),
                    k.shared_connection_count = 1,
                    k.sources = ['linkedin'],
                    k.last_interaction_at = date($connected_on),
                    k.interaction_count = 0
                ON MATCH SET
                    k.shared_connection_count = k.shared_connection_count + 1,
                    k.sources = CASE
                        WHEN NOT 'linkedin' IN coalesce(k.sources, [])
                        THEN coalesce(k.sources, []) + ['linkedin']
                        ELSE k.sources
                    END
                RETURN existed
                """,
                {
                    **row,
                    "member_name": member_name,
                    "connected_on": row["connected_on"] or "2020-01-01",
                },
            )
            records = [r async for r in result]
            if records and records[0].data().get("existed"):
                merged += 1
            else:
                imported += 1

        await _recompute_scores(session, member_name)

    return {
        "imported": imported,
        "merged": merged,
        "message": f"Processed {imported + merged} contacts for {member_name}",
    }


async def _recompute_scores(session, member_name: str) -> None:
    """Recompute strength on every KNOWS edge originating from this member."""
    from services.scoring_service import compute_strength

    records = await session.run(
        """
        MATCH (m:Person {full_name: $member_name})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
        OPTIONAL MATCH (other:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[:KNOWS]->(p)
        WITH m, k, p,
             k.connected_on AS conn_date,
             k.last_interaction_at AS last_touch,
             k.interaction_count AS interaction_count,
             p.role AS role,
             count(DISTINCT other) AS shared_count
        OPTIONAL MATCH (:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k2:KNOWS]->(p)
        WITH k, conn_date, last_touch, interaction_count, role, shared_count,
             count(DISTINCT k2.source_member) AS source_count
        RETURN id(k) AS rel_id, conn_date, last_touch, interaction_count,
               role, shared_count, source_count
        """,
        {"member_name": member_name},
    )

    async for record in records:
        data = record.data()
        strength = compute_strength(
            data["conn_date"],
            data["role"],
            data["shared_count"],
            data["source_count"],
            interaction_count=data.get("interaction_count") or 0,
            last_touch=data.get("last_touch"),
        )
        await session.run(
            "MATCH ()-[k:KNOWS]->() WHERE id(k) = $rel_id SET k.strength = $strength",
            {"rel_id": data["rel_id"], "strength": strength},
        )

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, File, Form, HTTPException, UploadFile

from routers.auth import verify_api_key
from services.ingestion_service import ingest_csv
from services.smart_ingestion_service import smart_ingest

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/connections", tags=["connections"])


@router.post("/import")
async def import_connections(
    member_name: str = Form(...),
    file: UploadFile = File(...),
    _key: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
):
    csv_text = (await file.read()).decode("utf-8-sig")
    result = await ingest_csv(member_name, csv_text)
    return result


@router.post("/import-smart")
async def import_smart(
    member_name: str = Form(...),
    files: list[UploadFile] = File(...),
    _key: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
):
    if len(files) > 5:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Maximum 5 files allowed per upload.")
    if not member_name.strip():
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="member_name is required.")

    file_pairs: list[tuple[str, bytes]] = []
    for f in files:
        raw = await f.read()
        file_pairs.append((f.filename or "unknown", raw))

    result = await smart_ingest(member_name.strip(), file_pairs)
    return result

content.text:
Everything is done and builds cleanly. Here's a summary of every change:

### Backend

**`backend/services/file_extraction.py`** (new)
- `.csv/.txt/.md/.ics` → UTF-8 decode
- `.eml` → Python `email` stdlib: headers + text body
- `.pdf` → `pypdf` page text
- Fallback: UTF-8, skip binary; 8 000 char cap per file

**`backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py`** (new)
- Builds prompt with all file text blocks
- Exposes `upsert_contact` and `upsert_interaction` as OpenAI-format tools to the LLM
- Agentic loop (up to 20 rounds): executes each tool call, feeds results back, waits for text summary
- Returns `{imported, merged, skipped, interactions, summary}`

**`backend/services/ingestion_service.py`** (modified)
- `upsert_contact()` — per-contact primitive; writes `sources`, `last_interaction_at`, `interaction_count` to `KNOWS`
- `upsert_interaction()` — per-interaction primitive; appends signal type to `sources`, bumps `interaction_count`
- `ingest_csv()` — same behavior, now also sets provenance fields
- `_recompute_scores()` — passes `interaction_count` and `last_touch` to scorer

**`backend/services/scoring_service.py`** (modified)
- New `interaction_freq` dimension (weight 10%): 0 → 0.5 → 1.0 at ≥2 and ≥5 interactions
- `recency_score` uses `last_touch` (overrides `connected_on`) when available
- Weights redistributed: recency 25%, shared connections 20%

**`backend/routers/connections.py`** (modified)
- `POST /api/connections/import-smart` — accepts up to 5 `UploadFile`s + `member_name`, delegates to `smart_ingest()`

**`backend/requirements.txt`** — added `pypdf==5.4.0`

### Frontend

**`frontend/app/components/WelcomeModal.tsx`** (new)
- Full-screen overlay on first load, asks for name, saves to `localStorage`, cannot be dismissed without a name

**`frontend/app/components/FileUploader.tsx`** (new, replaces `CSVUploader.tsx`)
- Up to 5 files, any extension; drag-and-drop; per-file remove; calls `/import-smart`; shows AI summary on result

**`frontend/app/components/ChatInterface.tsx`** (modified)
- On mount: reads `localStorage` → sets `sourceMember` or shows `WelcomeModal`
- "Acting as" top bar replaced with a slim read-only chip + "change" link
- `+` icon added to the left of the textarea; opens `FileUploader`
- "Import CSV" button removed

All four formats work perfectly. Here's what was created and what the LLM extracted:

| File | Format | Use case | Contacts | Interactions |
|------|--------|----------|----------|--------------|
| `data/sample_email_thread.eml` | EML | Forwarded email chain, To/Cc headers + body mentions | 6 | 3 |

content.text:
The issue is `Record<string, unknown>` clashing with JSX — casting `unknown` in JSX expressions confuses the compiler in some spots. Giving `ScoreCard` a proper interface:

The TypeScript error says "Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'ReactNode'". The issue is with the JSX comment `{/* Per-dimension bars */}` - that's weird. Let me look more carefully at the context. Actually the problem might be that `data` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>` and when used in JSX, the result type needs to be `ReactNode`. 

Actually wait, the error is pointing to the comment `{/* Per-dimension bars */}`. That's actually not the comment itself but that the TypeScript compiler says line 181 col 7 is the problem. Let me look at what's around that comment.
 
The error message is pointing to line 181, but that's likely just where TypeScript detected the issue - the actual problem could be in a nearby expression. Since `data` is `Record<string, unknown>`, something like `{data.last_touch as string}` might be causing the type mismatch when rendered in JSX. The line number might be misleading because TypeScript reports errors at the containing expression level rather than the exact problematic value. The real issue might be that `unknown` type doesn't have a defined truthy check in JSX conditionals, so the `{(data.interaction_count as number) > 0 || data.last_touch}` pattern is problematic. I think the cleanest solution is to define a proper typed interface for the ScoreCard component instead of relying on `Record<string, unknown>`.

content.text:
`interaction_count: 4` and `last_touch: 2026-04-16` are now in the response. Everything is wired up. Here's a summary of what was done and where interactions flow:

---

**Where interactions are stored**

`KNOWS` edges in Neo4j carry three provenance fields written by `ingestion_service.upsert_interaction`:
- `interaction_count` — total number of recorded touches
- `last_interaction_at` — date of the most recent one
- `sources` — list of signal types (e.g. `["csv", "email"]`)

---

**Where they're considered in scoring**

`scoring_service.py` turns those fields into two score contributions (weights sum to 1.0):

| Dimension | Weight | Signal used |
|---|---|---|
| Recency | 25% | `last_interaction_at` overrides `connected_on` if more recent |
| Interaction frequency | 10% | `interaction_count` → `0 / 0.5 / 1.0` at thresholds 0 / 2 / 5 |

---

**How to visualize them**

**Score card** (chat — ask "how strong is our relationship with Natasha Ivanova"): now shows all 5 bars (Recency, Seniority, Shared conns, **Interactions**, Src diversity) each with its weight label, plus an "N interactions · Last touch: YYYY-MM-DD" row at the bottom.

**Graph sidebar** (Graph tab → click any node): same breakdown with an info block showing interaction count and last touch date.

input.old_string: async def get_full_graph() -> dict:
input.new_string:
async def top_contacts(
    source_member: str | None = None, limit: int = 10
) -> list[dict]:
    """Top contacts ranked by relationship strength, optionally filtered to one member."""
    conditions = ["NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false)"]
    params: dict = {"limit": limit}
    if source_member:
        conditions.append("member.full_name = $source_member")
        params["source_member"] = source_member
    where = " AND ".join(conditions)
    cypher = f"""
    MATCH (member:Person {{is_tsi_member: true}})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
    WHERE {where}
    WITH p,
         collect(DISTINCT member.full_name) AS known_by,
         max(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) AS best_strength,
         sum(coalesce(k.interaction_count, 0)) AS total_interactions,
         max(k.last_interaction_at) AS last_touch
    ORDER BY best_strength DESC
    LIMIT $limit
    RETURN p.full_name AS name, p.company AS company, p.role AS role, p.id AS person_id,
           best_strength AS strength, total_interactions AS interaction_count,
           last_touch, known_by
    """
    return await run_query(cypher, params)


async def stale_relationships(
    months: int = 12, source_member: str | None = None
) -> list[dict]:
    """Contacts with no recorded touch in the last N months (relationship gone cold)."""
    params: dict = {"cutoff_days": months * 30}
    member_filter = ""
    if source_member:
        member_filter = "AND member.full_name = $source_member"
        params["source_member"] = source_member
    cypher = f"""
    MATCH (member:Person {{is_tsi_member: true}})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
    WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false) {member_filter}
    WITH p,
         collect(DISTINCT member.full_name) AS known_by,
         max(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) AS best_strength,
         max(k.last_interaction_at) AS last_touch,
         max(k.connected_on) AS connected_on
    WHERE last_touch IS NULL
       OR duration.between(date(last_touch), date()).days > $cutoff_days
    ORDER BY best_strength DESC
    RETURN p.full_name AS name, p.company AS company, p.role AS role, p.id AS person_id,
           best_strength AS strength, known_by,
           toString(last_touch) AS last_touch, toString(connected_on) AS connected_on
    """
    return await run_query(cypher, params)


async def team_coverage_stats() -> list[dict]:
    """Per-member stats: connection count, avg strength, top companies."""
    cypher = """
    MATCH (member:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
    WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false)
    WITH member,
         count(DISTINCT p) AS total_contacts,
         round(avg(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) * 1000) / 1000 AS avg_strength,
         sum(coalesce(k.interaction_count, 0)) AS total_interactions,
         collect(DISTINCT p.company)[0..5] AS top_companies
    RETURN member.full_name AS member_name,
           total_contacts, avg_strength, total_interactions, top_companies
    ORDER BY total_contacts DESC
    """
    return await run_query(cypher, {})


async def network_summary() -> dict:
    """High-level network health stats."""
    rows = await run_query(
        """
        MATCH (p:Person)
        WITH count(p) AS total_people,
             sum(CASE WHEN coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS members,
             sum(CASE WHEN NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS contacts
        MATCH (a:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k:KNOWS]->(b:Person)
        RETURN total_people, members, contacts,
               count(k) AS total_edges,
               round(avg(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) * 1000) / 1000 AS avg_strength,
               sum(coalesce(k.interaction_count, 0)) AS total_interactions,
               count(DISTINCT b.company) AS companies_covered
        """,
        {},
    )

    strong_rows = await run_query(
        """
        MATCH (member:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
        WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false)
        WITH p, max(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) AS s
        WHERE s >= 0.7
        RETURN count(p) AS strong_count
        """,
        {},
    )

    cold_rows = await run_query(
        """
        MATCH (member:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
        WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false)
          AND (k.last_interaction_at IS NULL OR
               duration.between(date(k.last_interaction_at), date()).days > 365)
        RETURN count(DISTINCT p) AS cold_count
        """,
        {},
    )

    top_companies = await run_query(
        """
        MATCH (member:Person {is_tsi_member: true})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
        WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false) AND p.company IS NOT NULL
        WITH p.company AS company, count(DISTINCT p) AS depth
        ORDER BY depth DESC LIMIT 8
        RETURN company, depth
        """,
        {},
    )

    if not rows:
        return {}
    r = rows[0]
    return {
        **r,
        "strong_relationships": strong_rows[0]["strong_count"] if strong_rows else 0,
        "cold_relationships": cold_rows[0]["cold_count"] if cold_rows else 0,
        "top_companies": top_companies,
    }


async def mutual_connections(
    target_a: str, target_b: str
) -> list[dict]:
    """People who are connected to BOTH target_a and target_b (by name/company fragment)."""
    cypher = """
    MATCH (a:Person)
    WHERE toLower(a.full_name) CONTAINS toLower($target_a)
       OR toLower(a.company) CONTAINS toLower($target_a)
    WITH a LIMIT 3
    MATCH (b:Person)
    WHERE toLower(b.full_name) CONTAINS toLower($target_b)
       OR toLower(b.company) CONTAINS toLower($target_b)
    WITH a, b LIMIT 3
    MATCH (bridge:Person)-[:KNOWS]-(a)
    MATCH (bridge)-[:KNOWS]-(b)
    WHERE bridge <> a AND bridge <> b
    RETURN DISTINCT bridge.full_name AS name, bridge.company AS company,
           bridge.role AS role, bridge.id AS person_id,
           coalesce(bridge.is_tsi_member, false) AS is_member
    ORDER BY is_member DESC
    LIMIT 10
    """
    return await run_query(cypher, {"target_a": target_a, "target_b": target_b})


async def recent_additions(
    days: int = 30, source_member: str | None = None
) -> list[dict]:
    """Contacts connected or interacted with in the last N days."""
    params: dict = {"cutoff_days": days}
    member_filter = ""
    if source_member:
        member_filter = "AND member.full_name = $source_member"
        params["source_member"] = source_member
    cypher = f"""
    MATCH (member:Person {{is_tsi_member: true}})-[k:KNOWS]->(p:Person)
    WHERE NOT coalesce(p.is_tsi_member, false) {member_filter}
      AND (
        (k.last_interaction_at IS NOT NULL AND
         duration.between(date(k.last_interaction_at), date()).days <= $cutoff_days)
        OR
        (k.connected_on IS NOT NULL AND
         duration.between(date(k.connected_on), date()).days <= $cutoff_days)
      )
    WITH p,
         collect(DISTINCT member.full_name) AS known_by,
         max(coalesce(k.strength, 0.0)) AS strength,
         max(k.last_interaction_at) AS last_touch,
         max(k.connected_on) AS connected_on
    RETURN p.full_name AS name, p.company AS company, p.role AS role, p.id AS person_id,
           strength, known_by,
           toString(last_touch) AS last_touch, toString(connected_on) AS connected_on
    ORDER BY last_touch DESC, connected_on DESC
    """
    return await run_query(cypher, params)

import httpx
from tools._config import backend_url, api_key


async def get_top_contacts(source_member: str = "", limit: int = 10) -> list[dict]:
    """Return the strongest relationships in the network ranked by score."""
    params: dict = {"limit": limit}
    if source_member:
        params["source_member"] = source_member
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as client:

            f"{backend_url}/api/graph/top-contacts",
            params=params,
            headers={"X-API-Key": api_key},
        )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json().get("results", [])

import os

from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

input.new_string:
import os

from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

from tools.import_connections import import_linkedin_connections
from tools.find_warm_path import find_warm_path
from tools.score_relationship import score_relationship_strength
from tools.search_graph import search_by_company_or_role
from tools.top_contacts import get_top_contacts
from tools.stale_relationships import get_stale_relationships
from tools.team_stats import get_team_coverage_stats
from tools.network_summary import get_network_summary
from tools.mutual_connections import get_mutual_connections
from tools.recent_additions import get_recent_additions

input.old_string:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="sse")
input.new_string:
@mcp.tool()
async def top_relationships(source_member: str = "", limit: int = 10) -> str:
    """List the strongest relationships in the network ranked by score.

    Args:
        source_member: Filter to one TSI team member's relationships (optional)
        limit: Number of contacts to return (default 10)
    """
    results = await get_top_contacts(source_member, limit)
    if not results:
        return "No contacts found."
    lines = [f"Top {len(results)} relationships:\n"]
    for r in results:
        known = ", ".join(r.get("known_by", []))
        interactions = r.get("interaction_count", 0)
        last = r.get("last_touch") or "—"
        lines.append(
            f"- {r['name']} ({r.get('role','N/A')} @ {r.get('company','N/A')}) "
            f"strength: {r.get('strength', 0):.2f}, interactions: {interactions}, "
            f"last touch: {last}, known by: {known}"
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


@mcp.tool()
async def cold_relationships(months: int = 12, source_member: str = "") -> str:
    """Find relationships that have gone cold — no interaction in the last N months.

    Args:
        months: Months of inactivity to flag as stale (default 12)
        source_member: Filter to one team member (optional)
    """
    results = await get_stale_relationships(months, source_member)
    if not results:
        return f"No stale relationships found (>{months} months inactive). Great coverage!"
    lines = [f"{len(results)} relationships inactive for >{months} months:\n"]
    for r in results:
        known = ", ".join(r.get("known_by", []))
        last = r.get("last_touch") or "never"
        lines.append(
            f"- {r['name']} ({r.get('role','N/A')} @ {r.get('company','N/A')}) "
            f"strength: {r.get('strength', 0):.2f}, last touch: {last}, known by: {known}"
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


@mcp.tool()
async def team_coverage() -> str:
    """Show per-team-member network stats: connection count, avg strength, total interactions."""
    results = await get_team_coverage_stats()
    if not results:
        return "No team member data found."
    lines = ["Team coverage breakdown:\n"]
    for r in results:
        companies = ", ".join(r.get("top_companies", []))
        lines.append(
            f"- {r['member_name']}: {r.get('total_contacts', 0)} contacts, "
            f"avg strength: {r.get('avg_strength', 0):.2f}, "
            f"interactions: {r.get('total_interactions', 0)}, "
            f"top companies: {companies or '—'}"
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


@mcp.tool()
async def network_health() -> str:
    """Overall network health dashboard: totals, company coverage, strong vs cold relationships."""
    d = await get_network_summary()
    if not d:
        return "No network data available."
    top = "\n".join(
        f"  {r['company']}: {r['depth']} contacts"
        for r in d.get("top_companies", [])
    )
    return (
        f"Network Summary\n"
        f"  Total contacts: {d.get('contacts', 0)}\n"
        f"  TSI members: {d.get('members', 0)}\n"
        f"  Companies covered: {d.get('companies_covered', 0)}\n"
        f"  Total relationship edges: {d.get('total_edges', 0)}\n"
        f"  Avg relationship strength: {d.get('avg_strength', 0):.2f}\n"
        f"  Strong relationships (≥0.7): {d.get('strong_relationships', 0)}\n"
        f"  Cold relationships (>12mo): {d.get('cold_relationships', 0)}\n"
        f"  Total recorded interactions: {d.get('total_interactions', 0)}\n"
        f"\nDeepest company coverage:\n{top}"
    )


@mcp.tool()
async def bridges_between(target_a: str, target_b: str) -> str:
    """Find people connected to both of two targets — useful to bridge two companies or people.

    Args:
        target_a: First person name or company fragment
        target_b: Second person name or company fragment
    """
    results = await get_mutual_connections(target_a, target_b)
    if not results:
        return f"No mutual connections found between '{target_a}' and '{target_b}'."
    lines = [f"People connected to both '{target_a}' and '{target_b}':\n"]
    for r in results:
        member_tag = " [TSI]" if r.get("is_member") else ""
        lines.append(
            f"- {r['name']}{member_tag} — {r.get('role','N/A')} @ {r.get('company','N/A')}"
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


@mcp.tool()
async def recent_activity(days: int = 30, source_member: str = "") -> str:
    """List contacts added or interacted with in the last N days.

    Args:
        days: Lookback window in days (default 30)
        source_member: Filter to one team member (optional)
    """
    results = await get_recent_additions(days, source_member)
    if not results:
        return f"No recent activity in the last {days} days."
    lines = [f"{len(results)} contacts active in the last {days} days:\n"]
    for r in results:
        known = ", ".join(r.get("known_by", []))
        touch = r.get("last_touch") or r.get("connected_on") or "—"
        lines.append(
            f"- {r['name']} ({r.get('role','N/A')} @ {r.get('company','N/A')}) "
            f"last touch: {touch}, known by: {known}"
        )
    return "\n".join(lines)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="sse")

content.text:
All done and pushed. Here's the full picture of what's now answerable:

**Existing tools (unchanged)**
| Question | Tool |
|---|---|
| "Find a warm intro to X at Y" | `warm_path` |
| "Who do we know at Goldman Sachs?" | `search_network` |
| "Show all CTOs / Partners we know" | `search_network(role=...)` |
| "How strong is our relationship with X?" | `score_relationship` |

**6 new tools (just added)**
| Question | Tool | Live data |
|---|---|---|
| "Who are our strongest contacts?" / "who has most interactions?" | `top_relationships` | James Mitchell @ Barclays: 0.90 |
| "Which relationships have gone cold?" | `cold_relationships` | 24 stale contacts flagged |
| "Who on the team has the biggest network?" | `team_coverage` | Xoyo: 37, Test User: 24… |
| "Network health overview" | `network_health` | 82 contacts, 44 companies, 19 strong, 44 cold |
| "Who knows both Goldman Sachs and TPG Capital?" | `bridges_between` | Xoyo (TSI member) |
| "Who have we added / met recently?" | `recent_activity` | 82 contacts in last 365 days |

All 6 are wired into:
- REST endpoints (`/api/graph/top-contacts`, `/stale`, `/team-stats`, `/summary`, `/mutual`, `/recent`)
- Chat LLM via `OPENAI_TOOLS` — the chat can now answer all of these from the `localhost:3000` UI
- MCP server — available to any MCP client (Cursor, Claude Desktop, etc.)

<user_query>
Sorry, I encountered an error: Object of type Date is not JSON serializable
</user_query>

The error "Object of type Date is not JSON serializable" is coming from the new Cypher queries. Neo4j returns `Date` objects from the driver, and when we try to serialize them to JSON, Python's `json.dumps` doesn't know how to handle them.

The issue is in the new functions in `graph_service.py`. The Cypher queries are returning date fields like `last_touch`, `connected_on` directly from the Neo4j driver as `neo4j.time.Date` objects, and we're not converting them to strings before returning.

In `get_score_details`, we added a `_fmt_date` helper, but for the new functions we're just returning raw results from `run_query` without converting dates.


I need to check how `run_query` handles the results to see where the conversion should happen.

input.query: How does run_query work and how are Neo4j results serialized?

content.text:
The root cause is clear — `top_contacts` returns `max(k.last_interaction_at)` as a raw Neo4j `Date` object without `toString()`. The fix is to serialize all temporal types centrally in `run_query` so this can never happen again:

The issue is clear. The new functions (`top_contacts`, `stale_relationships`, `recent_additions`) return raw `run_query` results which may contain Neo4j `Date` objects in `last_touch` and `connected_on` fields. Even though the Cypher uses `toString(last_touch)`, sometimes those fields come back as `None` (which is fine) or as Neo4j `Date` objects if they're not null and the `toString()` wrapping doesn't work because the field is null before the `toString()` call.

Wait, looking at the Cypher queries more carefully:


In `top_contacts`, the aggregated `max(k.last_interaction_at)` returns a raw Neo4j Date object without conversion. In `stale_relationships`, `toString()` is applied to the aggregated `last_touch` in the RETURN clause, which should handle the conversion for non-null values, but I need to verify whether the aggregation itself is causing issues with the type conversion. `toString()` to all date fields in the Cypher queries, or add a utility function to handle Neo4j date conversion in the results. The cleanest approach is to create a helper function that walks through the result dictionaries and converts any Neo4j temporal types to strings, then apply it in the query execution layer.

input.old_string:
async def run_query(cypher: str, params: dict | None = None) -> list[dict]:
    driver = await get_driver()
    async with driver.session() as session:
        result = await session.run(cypher, params or {})
        return [record.data() async for record in result]

content.text: Now that `run_query` handles all temporal types, the `_fmt_date` helper in `get_score_details` and the `toString()` calls in the Cypher queries are redundant but harmless — no need to touch them. Rebuild:

content.text: Fixed at the root. The `_coerce` helper in `run_query` now recursively converts any Neo4j `Date`, `DateTime`, `Time`, or `Duration` object to its string form before the dict leaves the database layer — so this class of error can't happen anywhere in the app going forward.

The user wants to add more interaction data to the graph. Currently, interactions are stored on `KNOWS` edges via `interaction_count`, `last_interaction_at`, and `sources`. 

The way to add more interaction data is to ingest more files via the `+` button in the UI, or we can create more sample interaction data files and ingest them programmatically.

content.text:
Done. Refresh `localhost:3000` and open a fresh chat. The empty state now shows:

**Left column — "Try asking"**: 8 example questions, one per tool, that populate the input box when clicked.

**Right column — "Available tools"**: a 2×5 grid of tool tiles. Hover any tile to see a tooltip with the full description of what that tool does and when to use it. Tools covered:

| Tile | What it does |
|---|---|
| 🔗 Warm path | 3-strategy intro chains |
| 🔍 Search network | Company/role lookup |
| 📊 Score relationship | 5-dimension strength breakdown |
| 🏆 Top contacts | Ranked by strength + interactions |
| 🧊 Cold relationships | Stale contact detection |
| 👥 Team coverage | Per-member stats |
| 🌐 Network health | Top-level dashboard |
| 🤝 Mutual connections | Bridge finder |
| 🕒 Recent activity | Last N days of touches |

Stage the listed unstaged changes, commit, and push for this branch.
- Current branch: main.
- Base branch: main.
- Stage all listed unstaged files before committing.
- Write a concise commit message.
- Push after creating the commit.
- Do not include files outside this list in the commit.

Unstaged files to stage:
- backend/requirements.txt (modified)
- backend/routers/connections.py (modified)
- backend/services/file_extraction.py (untracked)
- backend/services/ingestion_service.py (modified)
- backend/services/scoring_service.py (modified)
- backend/services/smart_ingestion_service.py (untracked)
- data/sample_calendar.ics (untracked)
- data/sample_contacts.md (untracked)
- data/sample_email_thread.eml (untracked)
- data/sample_meeting_notes.txt (untracked)
- frontend/app/components/FileUploader.tsx (untracked)
- frontend/app/components/WelcomeModal.tsx (untracked)

Add LLM-powered universal ingestion pipeline and onboarding UI

- file_extraction.py: extract text from CSV, EML, ICS, PDF, TXT, MD
- smart_ingestion_service.py: agentic LLM loop calling upsert_contact/
  upsert_interaction tools to populate graph from any file type
- ingestion_service.py: add upsert_contact and upsert_interaction
  primitives; add sources, last_interaction_at, interaction_count to
  KNOWS edges for richer scoring
- scoring_service.py: add interaction_frequency_score (10% weight);
  use last_touch to override recency if more recent than connected_on
- connections.py: new POST /api/connections/import-smart endpoint
- WelcomeModal.tsx: first-load modal asking for user name, persisted
  to localStorage; replaces editable "Acting as" input bar
- FileUploader.tsx: universal file uploader (up to 5 any-extension
  files) triggered by + icon in chat input bar
- Sample data files: EML, ICS, TXT, MD for testing all ingest paths
- pypdf added to requirements for PDF extraction
EOF
)" && git push
